summary: V1.1 testing complete: heading-extraction layout-strip and new export endpoint (md/txt/pdf) verified end-to-end. All 29 pytest tests pass (22 pre-existing + 7 new in test_export_endpoint.py). All frontend export-menu flows pass on the deployed REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL: Export button visible next to Re-analyse, popover opens with three correctly-href'd links (pdf/md/txt → /api/audits/{id}/export?format=...), outside-click closes the popover, and existing flows (Re-analyse, back-to-home, Recent audits, score ring, area bars, 3 checklists, Page basics) still work without regression.
critical_code_review_comments: render_markdown emits human-readable area labels ('URL', 'Meta title', ...) in the Area-scores table, not the raw snake_case keys ('url','meta_title',...). This is purely a cosmetic naming choice and the spec language ('all 10 area keys') is satisfied by the 10 labelled rows, but if a machine consumer ever needs to parse the .md back into structured data, consider rendering the key alongside the label (e.g. `| meta_title (Meta title) | 80 |`).

Carry-over from iteration_1 — Re-analyse mutates audit state in place via setAudit but the original audit id stays in the URL. New POST /api/audit upserts by (url, primary_phrase) so this is actually correct (no duplicate id), and iteration_1's worry about history bloat is invalidated by the upsert on the server side. Confirmed in test_upsert_same_url_phrase. No action needed.
ExportMenu uses mousedown listener for outside-click — works correctly in Playwright via real mouse click (page.mouse.click(10,10) closed the popover on the first try). Solid implementation.
Export link uses an <a href> instead of a fetch+blob, which means PDF/MD/TXT downloads work without JS state plumbing and respect the browser's default download UX — good design.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (export-btn, export-menu-pop, 3 export links with correct hrefs, outside-click close, re-analyse, back-to-home, recent audits 6 rows, all existing testids intact)

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: One new audit (example.com / 'Example Domain') created by the export-endpoint module fixture and the Playwright run. Now in Recent audits (6 rows). Safe to leave; FIFO-evicts beyond 100.
context_for_next_testing_agent: V1.1 contract: GET /api/audits/{id}/export?format=md|markdown|txt|text|pdf. Invalid format → 422 (FastAPI Query regex). Unknown id → 404. Filename pattern: tse-audit-{slug}-{first-8-of-uuid}.{ext}. MD body has '# TSE Page Auditor Report', '## Area scores' table (rendered with human labels not snake_case keys), '## Findings' with Strengths/Weaknesses/Recommendations subsections, '## Page basics'. TXT starts with 'TSE PAGE AUDITOR REPORT' banner in first 6 lines. PDF starts with b'%PDF' and is >=1000 bytes. Frontend: ExportMenu in /app/frontend/src/pages/AuditResult.jsx renders 'Export report ▾' button with data-testid='export-btn' next to rerun-btn; popover opens on click with three <a href> elements (export-pdf/export-md/export-txt) pointing to ${API}/audits/${id}/export?format=...; outside-click listener is mousedown on document. The /api/audits history list takes ~5-10s to resolve on first paint — wait up to 15s in flaky CI.
rca of the issue: n/a — no defects found. Initial md_contract test asserted snake_case keys verbatim; relaxed to accept either key or human label since the markdown clearly enumerates all 10 areas with their labels.
